mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, indicating the signal fails to capture labor market deterioration.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline with statistical significance (p > 0.05) across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during supply-constrained periods, producing negative returns in >50% of observations when capacity utilization exceeds 80%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation acceleration above 0.4% forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation pass-through successfully, yielding positive returns in >65% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when labor force participation drops below 60%, causing signal accuracy to fall below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above trend reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit margins, which translates into sustained positive equity returns over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: GDP revisions consistently downgrade initial estimates, causing the signal to misprice forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 4-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and weakening business investment, which reduces earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of double-dip production months due to financial market decoupling from real economy indicators.
